While we are unable to extend an offer of admission to you at this time, you were among the best of our applicants and we hope that we will be able to offer you admission at a later date.

The timing of our final admissions decision will depend upon when we hear from the students already admitted. Often we don’t hear decisions from admitted students until the April 15 response deadline, so we may not be able to give you a final decision about your waitlist status until that date. Once we have heard from the majority of the admitted students, we will begin contacting waitlisted candidates directly. This year, the Graduate School received over 10,000 applications for our Masters and Ph.D. programs. As you can understand, the selection process for admission to all of our programs is very competitive, and given the nature of graduate study, the number of students admitted to our programs must be severely limited. As a result, the Admissions Committee was forced to make many difficult choices among a pool of highly accomplished applicants.

records.notes: Dear Loser, I am the Director of Graduate Admissions and Placement at the University of Texas at Austin's Department of Government. Although we are unable to admit you at this time to our Ph.D. program, I am happy to inform you that the admissions committee was impressed by your application. We have placed you on a waitlist for Fall 2023, in the hope that some spots will open up in our incoming class. Our admissions process is very competitive, and the decisions are tailored as much to the mix of interests represented in the class as they are to the quality of the applicants. We do believe you could be successful here if it turns out we are able to admit you. If you have general requirements or process-related questions about our program, please feel free to contact our Graduate Coordinator, Chaz Nailor (cnailor@austin.utexas.edu). Although unfortunately we could not admit you outright, we would very much like you to keep our program in mind, as you make decisions about your graduate career. We hope to make our decisions about the waitlisted candidates in late March/early April as we hear from our current admits, and definitely before April 15.

I just thought I'd share my results over here, hoping it may give some people hope that Yale is starting to get things rolling despite the radio silence. If you don't hear back from the graduate admissions or department, then you're probably going to hear from your POI informally as I did. Or maybe this was a one off, I'm not sure. Regardless, I'm elated. Good luck again everyone :)

Goodluck everyone and dont stress :)

Got an incredibly nice email from my POI yesterday.
